pr.title: Restore ability to build with Make in nix shell
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Restored Make-based builds inside the Nix shell by adding the minimal environment and filesystem setup that the non-Nix Make flow expects: a tools/mwcc_compiler symlink to the Nix-provided MWCC GC compiler directory, plus DEVKITPPC and WINE environment variables in the shell override. The PR notes this was easy to keep supported because it only required env vars and the compiler symlink.

what_changed.change: Added a postPatch hook that creates tools/mwcc_compiler as a symlink to ${mwcc}/GC.
Existing shellHook runs postPatch, so the symlink setup is also applied when entering the development shell.
Added DEVKITPPC = pkgs.devkitppc to the Nix shell override.
Added WINE = "${pkgs.wibo}/bin/wibo" to expose the expected Wine-compatible runner for Make builds.

smart_moves: Kept Make support in the Nix shell with a very small compatibility layer instead of duplicating or rewriting the build flow.
Used Nix-provided package paths for MWCC, devkitPPC, and wibo, preserving reproducibility while satisfying Make's expected environment.
Put compiler symlink creation in postPatch and relied on shellHook invoking runHook postPatch, so the same setup can apply both to derivation patching and interactive shell use.
Used a symlink rather than copying the compiler tree, avoiding redundant toolchain materialization.
decomp_lessons: When preserving legacy Make workflows inside Nix, identify the exact filesystem paths and environment variables the Makefiles expect, then shim only those pieces.
Interactive Nix shells may need to run derivation setup hooks such as postPatch if local tools expect generated or linked paths in the working tree.
For decomp projects with multiple build frontends, small compatibility hooks can reduce breakage while the project transitions tooling.
Build environment fixes can be valuable even when they do not touch game code, because reproducible local builds directly affect matching and review velocity.
naming_conventions: The Make-compatible compiler path remains tools/mwcc_compiler.
DEVKITPPC is exported in uppercase, matching the conventional devkitPPC environment variable expected by build tooling.
WINE is exported in uppercase and points to the wibo executable path, matching the apparent Make/toolchain expectation for a Wine-like command.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Observed that duplicated CI/build setup was accumulating and suggested considering Nix for all CI, including possibly adding a Make matrix entry for Nix.

searchable_terms: Restore ability to build with Make in nix shell
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that add or discuss a Nix Make CI matrix entry.
Find other PRs touching tools/mwcc_compiler or MWCC path setup.
Find Nix shell changes involving DEVKITPPC, WINE, wibo, or devkitppc.
Check whether later tooling consolidated Make, Ninja, Docker, and Nix CI duplication.
